content: The rise in sea levels at the end of the last Ice Age, around 12,000 years ago, exposed the shallow seaways that connected the British Isles to mainland Europe, allowing humans to migrate and establishing the modern coastline.
raw_content: The rise in sea levels at the end of the last Ice Age, around 12,000 years ago, exposed the shallow seaways that connected the British Isles to mainland Europe, allowing humans to migrate and establishing the modern coastline.
